---
title: "Setting up Shinylive and Quarto for teaching effenciecy"
draft: true
---

Implementing user interaction into static web pages are now possible
thanks to shiny and web assembly = shinylive.

## Settig up Shinylive^[https://github.com/quarto-ext/shinylive?tab=readme-ov-file#installation]

## 1. Create a vrtual environnement

  ```sh
  python3 -m venv .venv
  ```

  ```sh
  source .venv/bin/activate 
  ```

## 2. [install shinylive](https://github.com/quarto-ext/shinylive?tab=readme-ov-file#installation)

  ```sh
  pip install shinylive --upgrade
  ```

## 3. [use shinylive in quarto document](https://github.com/quarto-ext/shinylive?tab=readme-ov-file#usage)

To use this extension in your Quarto project, run this in the top level of your Quarto project:

  ```sh
  quarto add quarto-ext/shinylive
  ```


## References:

- [shinylive](https://shiny.posit.co/py/get-started/shinylive.html)
- [deploying shiny app with quarto](https://shiny.posit.co/py/get-started/shinylive.html#deploying-shinylive-applications)
- [shinylive github repo](https://github.com/quarto-ext/shinylive)
- [shiny for python web page](https://shiny.posit.co/py/)
